choices: The noblewoman, coming to claim her potion
A clever thief looking for valuables when the potion catches her eye
An elven sorceress looking to experiment in exotic magics
A halfling ranger and her orc warrior girlfriend looking for healing potions
Something else
1.content:
Nestled adjacent to the dusty streets of the town of Durmrin, Rizzik sat in his small alchemy shop, his nose buried in a large tome and his torso hunched over a workstation with a most curious-looking vial of liquid.

Having aspired since his youth to become a court wizard, Rizzik had dedicated most of his life to the study of the world of the arcane. Sadly, he possessed little innate magical ability, and while he petitioned the Elder Council of Magicians on several occasions to accept his application to the High Wizard Institute, he had neither the pedigree nor the talent to impress the stodgy old conjurers.

Despite reaching an impasse in the cultivation of any natural magical power, Rizzik found an outlet much more accessible: that of potionmaking. Relying more on science than on nature, the discipline was not considered the most prestigious among the magic-wielding community, but it was still widely popular. There was no lack of citizenry, regardless of social or economic standing, who were drawn to the notion of having a little bit of portable sorcery they could carry around in their pockets.

It was one such citizen who had submitted the special order which Rizzik was now focused on fulfilling. The Lady Estrel Ravenwind, younger sister of the Baroness Ravenwind, had heard rumor of Rizzik and his shop, which she visited the week prior. As word had spread of Rizzik’s willingness to provide certain elixirs not normally available through the set-in-their-ways court wizards, he began receiving an increased number of requests from nobility, the Lady Ravenwind far from the least among them. Arriving in an earthen-brown cloak and hood to appear inconspicuous (“Always about appearances with these folk,” Rizzik thought wryly), she had been up front about her request.

“Wizard, I want a potion to make me beautiful. I’m not talking about these paltry salves found in any shop that simply deepen the hue of hair or erase a bothersome pimple. I want your best work, a complete, full-body transformation. I wish to be the envy of every lady of the court and have every male gaze fixed on me. I am paying you a significant sum in advance, so I expect this to be your highest priority. I will return in one week to claim my purchase and provide the second half of your payment.”

Despite his own personal opinions on the vanity of the nobility and that the Lady Ravenwind was not particularly ugly to begin with (perhaps just slightly plain-looking), Rizzik was keenly aware of the importance of the request, not just for the satisfaction of his patronage but his own reputation. Over the next several days, he delved deep into his repertoire to perfect the mixture. Now, with time nearly up, he made his last adjustments. “Essence of dourvine...two pinches of vendiroot...lightly swirl...and...”

He stared at the vial in anticipation as the last of the ingredients settled to the bottom and slowly dissolved. When the last flake of material had dissipated, the entire liquid brightened with a reddish-pink glow, illuminating Rizzik’s excited face.

“Success!” He crowed. “The dourvine must have activated the inert ether present in the saturated quillasp venom I added yesterday! It’s the only way this specific color could have been produced! This should provide considerable enhancement to all desirable traits of the female form. The Lady Ravenwind will be most pleased with her elixir, but before she arrives, I must record my findings! The Council can’t ignore me forever, especially with a breakthrough like this!”

The giddy wizard rushed toward his desk upstairs, hastily closing the door behind him; however, in his eagerness he closed it a bit too hard. The rather rickety walls of his small shop shook from the impact, giving a shelf next to the workbench a small jolt. That jolt caused a rack of various test tubes of liquid to topple over, spilling the contents of each and causing them to coalesce and splash right into the vial on the bench. The vial inside reacted immediately, violently bubbling and foaming as it threatened to overflow from the top of the container. Small sparks spit out sporadically and the color shifted across nearly the entire visible spectrum, but then the liquid subsided and returned to its original consistency and hue. The entire rack slipped off the shelf and onto the darkened floor, out of view. Rizzik, too absorbed in his notes to hear anything from upstairs, continued his writing unwavering.

A few quiet minutes passed, until the front door of the shop slowly creaked open, revealing...
1.title: The Wizard’s Accidental Concoction

choices: Take a Spoonful of the Potion, as Rizzik Recommended
Ignore the Wizard's Advice and Drink Several Sips of the Elixir!
Test the Potion on One of her Servants Before Drinking it Herself
Leave the Room for a Moment, Allowing Someone Else to Steal it!
Her Sister, Baroness Ravenwind, Barges in to Interrupt Before She Can Decide!
11.content:
A familiar cloaked figure swept in through the doorway. After a quick glance around the shop, the Lady Ravenwind called out for Rizzik.

“Wizard, my potion better be among these vials! Your week is up, so I expect spectacular results!”

As he was putting the finishing touches on his notes, Rizzik heard the mildly irritated feminine voice echoing up the staircase. Filled with equal parts trepidation and excitement, her call provided the catalyst for him to rush back down the stairs. Nearly tripping over his robes, he caught himself on the counter, and hoisted the potion with a practiced flourish.

“Your beauty potion is quite ready, milady!” he exclaimed, giving a slight bow. Of course he only cared for her money and potential spreading of his reputation, but a little pomp and circumstance couldn’t hurt. “This potent brew is guaranteed to make you even more astounding, everyone in the kingdom will be in awe! Start with a spoonful and you should receive the results you desire almost instantaneously, though if even that isn’t enough for your loveliness that’s why I’m providing a full flask.”

Estrel’s eyes shone as she saw the realization of her vain desires in the reddish liquid. Rizzik had said exactly what she wanted to hear. Without hesitation, she threw another sack of gold on the counter and snatched the potion.

“Lovely! Enjoy your gold, and if this works as well as you claim I may just send other unfortunate ladies your way.”

Before Rizzik had a chance to respond, she rushed out of the shop and headed immediately back to her estate. She furtively scurried towards her chambers, taking care not to be seen by anyone carrying the potion. When at last she arrived in the safety of her own room, she cast off the cloak and triumphantly placed the potion on a dresser.

“At last, I’ll be able to show up my sister, and every other lady at court for that matter!” she thought eagerly, before deciding to...
11.title: The Potion is Purchased by its Patroness

choices: Beautiful! She Couldn't Wait to Show Off Her New Body, & Maybe Grow Some More...
Empowered! With the Potion, She Planned to Seize Control of the Whole Kingdom!
Horrified! She Rushes Back to Rizzik to Demand he Reverse her Growth!
111.content:
Estrel Ravenwind considered herself to be quite intelligent, she wasn’t going to fall prey to the mistake in so many stories of ignoring magical counsel. She was confident in Rizzik’s work - and if not she could deal with him later. For now, she would merely take a spoonful as he instructed and fill her form with the divine beauty she deserved.

She stripped to her undergarments and stood in front of one of the many full-size mirrors in her chambers. She wanted to savor saying goodbye to her plainess. Chuckling to herself, Estrel measured out a spoonful of the elixir, carefully resealed the flask, and then downed the brew.

At once a delightful, and mildly arousing, warmth spread throughout her body, not unlike drinking a high-quality mead. The feeling intensified in her breasts, buttocks, and face, and as she gazed on, the reflection looking back became subtly less familiar and more attractive. Her breasts, previously rather small, began to press out of her top as they expanded. More and more of the pale flesh of her behind was exposed as it strained against the rest of her clothing. Any imperfection in complexion cleared and her slight chubbiness melted away. Brown hair lengthened and shone into a rich auburn, and a new vitality filled her features as her legs lengthened an inch. The lady Ravenwind rubbed her hands over her new body blissfully, delighted by how incredibly beautiful she had become.

Rizzik’s potion had worked to exactly match her expectations.

But it wasn’t done working either.

As she prepared to at last tear her gaze away and redress, the warmth intensified to a burning heat. At first it was uncomfortable and Estrel began to call out for help, before it was replaced with a sensual pleasure, the cry in her throat morphing into a moan. Suddenly overcome with delight she closed her eyes, moaning and continuing to rub a body that was hardening and lengthening. While the potion had initially slimmed down her form, she now bulked up in an enhanced way. In addition to prodigious curves expanding again, toned muscle surfaced all over the noblewoman’s body.

“Mmmmm…. Mmmmm don’t stooopppp… Yeeeessssss…” she stuttered out as her entire body grew. Slowly but steadily she expanded, the fabric of her undergarments straining at the seams. As her breasts grew with her the top portion tore off altogether exposing even more of her glorious beauty. Finally, the effects of the potion wore off, leaving the Lady Estrel Ravenwind just over 7 feet tall: the most beautiful, tallest, and strongest lady in all the land.

As she opened her eyes and looked over these changes, she felt...
111.title: Lady Ravenwind Becomes a Towering Regal Beauty!

choices: The Next Evening, Estrel Demonstrates her New Size & Beauty at the Ball
Before the Ball, Baroness Ravenwind Discovers Her Sister's Transformation...
Estrel Becomes Addicted to Growing and Takes Even More of the Potion!
1111.content:
As the pleasurable warmth ebbed from her amazonian body, Estrel slowly opened her eyes. For a long moment, she gazed at the enhanced reflection in the mirror. As her caramel-colored eyes drank in the beautiful, toned, curvaceous body in front of her, her lips curled into a smile. There was only one way to describe what she was thinking:

“Beautiful!” she exclaimed, rubbing her breasts together. “No one else in the world holds a candle to the flame of my beauty! I didn’t expect being taller to be so incredible either, and I feel so strong! Perhaps that wizard deserves a bonus for fulfilling my desires so completely, but for now I must prepare to flaunt this superiority at the ball Duke Blakeney is hosting tomorrow.”

“First though…” she mused, glancing down at the few tatters still attempting to cover her body, “this remaining fabric is dreadfully tight and must be disposed of!”

Lady Ravenwind attempted to tug the offending undergarments off of her. Yet despite the newfound strength in her arms, her efforts were fruitless. The fabric was thoroughly clamped down on her muscular calves, barely covering her womanhood.

“Well,” she said with a wry smile, “I don’t believe it would hurt to grow out of this problem. I wouldn’t wish to frighten the other nobles by bringing too much of me, but perhaps another half a foot will be adequate”.

Running some quick calculations in her head (as a noblewoman she had been tutored in mathematics, after all) Estrel determined she’d grown around two feet from the spoonful. Naturally this meant a little under a quarter spoonful would suit her purposes. Just as excited to experience growth again as she was to remove her uncomfortable underwear, she greedily measured out the dosage and swallowed the bright liquid.

Faster than before, the delectable warmth permeated her cells and every part of her expanded. Her face could not become any more beautiful, but there was still room for her curves to expand, muscles to strengthen, and of course, height to increase. She began moaning once more, and as she passed seven and a half feet her garments lost their fight and fell off with a startling *snap!*.

“Oooooohhh that’s it… mmmmmm… but it still feels soooo goooooddd to use this potion.”

Her nether regions exposed, Estrel dropped a hand to stimulate herself. Her moans intensified as she felt the pleasure build, leaving her oblivious to the fact that she was still growing more than expected. At last, she reached a climax, and the tension left her body as she thrust past 8 feet in height.

“T’was more than I anticipated, perhaps the potion gains potency with the power of the user? Regardless, I can’t be upset when it was so magnificent, and the pleasure lingers even now. Though at this wondrous size my wardrobe is a new problem, so time to call for the tailor!”
1111.title: There's no Problem too Big to be Outgrown!

choices: Treyna deploys a smokebomb and escapes
Cornered with no other options, Treyna submits and is arrested
Treyna bargains with the captain to let her go
Disguised as a guard, Treyna’s partner creates a diversion
In desperation, Treyna takes a swig of the mysterious potion
12.content:
Clad in many pockets, a dark cloak, and a hood covering all bit a wisp of short red hair, the faint outline of a young woman appeared in the partially open doorway. Treyna Karsh, #3 on the Principality of Geralin’s Most Wanted list, crept noiselessly past the threshold, making sure she stuck to the shadows cast throughout the sparsely lit main floor of the shop. Though it was her first time inside, she was hardly unfamiliar with it, having scoped out the building for weeks. She had determined it was the most popular shop in town—hence, the most well-endowed with coin—based on the volume of foot traffic, but she also knew most of that traffic did not show up until late morning, especially on particularly cold days like today. This left her the perfect window of time to graciously relieve the poor wizard of his burdensome gold.

Keeping a close eye and ear on every door, Treyna tiptoed up to the front counter and slipped behind it, smiling expectantly as she spotted the many drawers lining its backside. One by one, she slowly inched each drawer open and peered inside. Though she had her trusty lockpicking tools at the ready, the thief became more and more surprised at not needing to use them. It soon became painfully apparent why as every single compartment was revealed to be empty.

Unbeknownst to her, Rizzik had relocated the gold he usually kept behind the counter to his safe in his office upstairs earlier that morning. His tills had already gotten quite full from the generous partial payment left to him by the Lady Ravenwind last week and he wanted to make sure he had it available in its entirety in case the worst came to pass and he had to provide her a refund.

Treyna slumped to the ground in disgust. Her stop in this small, backwater town, all her surveillance work on this surprisingly enticing target—all of it had been for naught. Just then, to add to her woes, she heard footsteps beginning to descend the stairs from the top floor. Jolting to her feet, she glanced begrudgingly in the direction of the sound. “I’ll be damned if I came all this way for nothing,” she vowed to herself.

Her gaze swept across the room and was immediately caught by a soft glow on a nearby workbench: a potion of some kind, though unlike any she had ever seen. It took no time at all to make her decision as she swooped over, snatched the vial, stuck it in a belt pocket, and slithered back through the front door. Her movements were fluid and unbroken throughout, a testament to her immense talent as a burglar.

Ambling furtively down the road away from the shop while making sure to maintain a balance of haste and anonymity, Treyna paused and glanced behind her, confirming her escape was clean. With a sigh, she straightened up in relief and turned back to continue, but froze in her tracks as she was suddenly surrounded by a circle of armor. Each guard brandished sharp metal in her direction, causing her to raise her hands away from her body and eye them warily. “Well, if it isn’t Treyna Karsh,” a voice familiar to her rose from behind the wall of bodies. Through it stepped Dara Quin, Captain of the Geralinean Guard. Her tall, well-built stature, gleaming breastplate with dark-red sash, and blonde hair tied smartly in a bun made her an imposing presence to most any criminal, but to Treyna, the extensive cat-and-mouse game they had long played had worn off some of that sheen.

“Really, Dara?” Treyna inclined her head wryly. “You chase me all the way out here, for what, a few scraps of food off a merchant cart?”

“And the Duchess’s pearl earrings, and Sir Worthington’s prized horse...” Dara replied, holding up her fingers in succession. “I could go on, but I’d rather start our journey back to the Onyx City dungeons before the solstice next month.”

Her barb caused a light chuckle throughout her rank of guards. Treyna’s eyebrows lifted in mock surprise. “Wow, did the Crown raise your wages recently? Sounds like you finally earn enough to pay your troops to think you’re funny.”

Dara shot back a half-smirk. “Honest pay, to clean the filth off the streets. Now...” she unsheathed her own sword and held it low but ready at her side, all feigned warmth gone from her face. “Come along. No need to make a scene, especially not a gruesome one.”

Treyna’s eyes darted to each other pair locked on her. Cursing herself for her lapse in awareness that lead to this position, her mind raced with the options now pressing upon her: fight, flee, or...feint?
12.title: Treyna’s Tight Spot

choices: To fulfill a bounty, Gresha and Maribel hunt the dreaded Arix beast
Gresha and Maribel explore a mysterious cave
Gresha and Maribel stumble across a bandit camp
Zevine spots a small puddle of spilled potion on the workbench and is curious...
Somethjng else
14.content:
The outlines of two figures filled the entrance to Rizzik’s shop, then stepped into the candlelight concentrated at the center of the room as the door closed behind them. Most anyone who hadn’t seen them before would have agreed they made the most peculiar pair, for they seemed nearly the opposite of each other in every way.

Though she was by far the shortest of the two at just over 3 feet tall, Maribel Flindlemar easily stood out the most. With large green eyes, scattered freckles, and sky blue hair tied up in twin buns straddling the top of her head, she was a veritable ball of energy. Many folk who were uneducated in the existence and prevalence of halflings would have mistaken the young woman for an 6-year-old girl. While this famously would have offended many of her kind, Maribel was not bothered by it; in fact, she didn’t seem to be bothered by anything. Everywhere she went, she radiated her sunny, spunky disposition to the delight—and occasionally the annoyance—of those around her. Those who thought her as weak as a child were soon shown their misconception, however. With a custom-made crossbow, a quiver full of bolts, and a sure aim, she held her own among adventurers, to the surprise of many of her contemporaries.

In the starkest of contrasts, to her side sulked the orc Gresha, of clan Volmogh. Standing at 6’8” with a solid, slightly stocky build, forest-green skin, and jet-black hair pulled into a high-hanging ponytail, she cancelled out any natural magnetism Maribel carried, especially with her eyes sunken into an almost-perpetual glare and accentuated by her snout-like nose and jutting mandibles. Though she had a look and countenance common to most orcs, her proximity to and tolerance of her counterpart made it stand out all the more. A quick temper and a sharp battleaxe rounded out her fearsome aura, one that few desired to question or challenge.

Maribel’s eyes twinkled as they darted back and forth between the stocked shelves on each side of the room. “Ooo, what do ya think of this place, Greshie?” she asked in a sweet, singsong tone. “We should be able to get plenty here!”

Gresha merely crossed her arms and grunted in response, perusing the shelves herself but looking much more bored in doing so.

“Wonder where the shopkeep is?” The halfing peered over the top of the front counter. Using her tiny knuckles she rapped lightly on its surface. “Helloooo...anyone here? Could we get some service, pretty please?”

After a few silent seconds, a large green fist slammed onto the counter next to her, causing all the vials on the shelves to shudder in place. “SERVICE!!” Gresha roared, as Maribel stifled a giggle.

Upstairs, the sound caused Rizzik to violently flinch in his chair and nearly spill the inkpot sitting on his desk. Gathering himself, he sighed in annoyance at the interruption. “A brutish voice like that obviously didn’t come from the Lady Ravenwind. Must be some other customer.” Turning to the side of the desk, he grasped a long metal tube feeding into the floor. “Zevine,” he spoke into the end, “could you handle the customer at the front?”

The other end of the tube was in the cellar where a peasant girl sat at a small desk, reading a large alchemy tone. Looking up, she marked her spot in the book and closed it, standing and leaning toward the source of the voice. “Right away, sir,” she replied.

The 18-year-old Zevine, Rizzik’s assistant he had hired a few years back, was plain and unassuming, yet endlessly curious, which drew her strongly to the study of alchemy. Within a few months, she had gained enough knowledge to run the shop while Rizzik was off restocking his ingredients, though would often wished she could join him, or help him mix one of his special orders. Climbing the flight of stairs, she opened the door and stepped behind the counter, greeting both patrons with a ready smile. “Good morning! What can I get you?”

Maribel grinned excitedly. “I wanna tryyy...two of those, one of those, maybe that thin purple one, and—”

“Healing potions,” Gresha interjected flatly.

“Awww,” Maribel pouted, “but I thought we could—”

“Healing potions.”

The halfling fluttered her lips with a puff of air in exasperation and threw up her hands. “Fine. We’ll just get five healing potions, the ten-gerling ones. Grumpy Greshie ruins the fun again,” she muttered under her breath. Gresha gave no reply, but the slightest hint of a smile briefly passed over her mouth.

“Of course,” Zevine nodded courteously as she bent down to retrieve the vials from a nearby shelf. To reach them in the back, she had to set aside a few other vials on the adjacent workbench, leading her to absently move the vial already sitting there closer to the edge to make room. This brought it within Maribel’s field of vision for the first time since she had entered and it immediately caught her eye.

“So shiny!” She marveled, pointing up to it. “Hey lady, can one of our healing potions be that one?”

Zevine straightened back up to look where she was pointing, regarding the potion for the first time. “Oh I don’t know if that’s...” she began, before staring closer at the liquid in increasing puzzlement. Turning back and picking up one of the healing potions, she set them on the bench side by side. They looked very similar, yet one seemed to glow with a slightly brighter sheen. From her reading, Zevine knew the reddish pink hue of healing potions were very unique among alchemical formulae, and knowing Rizzik, he probably forgot to return it to the shelf with the others. “Very well,” she finally answered, grouping the vial with four others and sliding them across the bench. “There you are! That’ll be fifty gerlings.”

Gresha fished the currency out of her pack and set it on the table, gathering and stowing the potions in return. Exchanging brief pleasantries with Zevine, she exited the shop and started down the road, with Maribel skipping along behind her.

“So...” the shorter woman leaned over to her companion, looking up at her in anticipation. Where to next?”

Gresha continued to stare straight ahead as they reached the edge of town and faced the cobblestone path meandering toward the horizon. Finally, she glanced down at Maribel and shrugged. “Out.”
14.title: Gresha and Maribel

choices: She gives her a normal potion, keeping the special one for herself
She gives her the special potion and Gresha takes a quick drink
She gives her the special potion and...whoa, Gresha drinks the whole thing!?
Jesarin recovers and attacks Maribel from behind
Something else
143.content:
“C’mon Greshie, let me hold the pack!” Maribel prodded her travel companion as they walked the winding path through the forest on the outskirts of Durmrin. “You’re always doing all the heavy lifting, but I want to contribute more! I’ve already proven I can do so many things, like scouting, hunting...”

“Sampling potions?” Gresha finished, with the almost imperceptible sliver of amusement her gruff orcish tone could muster.

Maribel sighed. “That was one time! —ok maybe two times, but it’s their own fault for being cherry flavored! C’mon, pleeeeeease?”

Gresha rolled her eyes and slipped the pack off her shoulder, handing it to the halfling. Maribel gave a short squeal of delight and happily draped the strap over her torso. Her peppy gait was suddenly halted however when an arrow punctured the ground right at their feet.

With no sound warning of their approach, two trios of armed men emerged from the trees at their flanks while two other figures came into view from the other side of a hill straight ahead of them. Gresha tensed, grabbing her axe from behind her back and swiveling her head to each side to keep everyone in her peripherals. “Bandits,” she sneered, spitting on the ground.

One of the figures in front of them, an elven woman with stripes of face paint across her cheeks, wearing furs, and leaning on a battlemage staff, stepped forward with a triumphant grin. “Greetings, travelers!” The woman announced loudly. “I am Jesarin, queen of the woodlands, and I—”

“Where’s your castle?” Maribel piped in, genuinely curious.

Jesarin shot her a brief glare, but forced a smile. “The forest is my castle little one, and you—”

“No, but seriously. I don’t see any buildings around here. Does it rise out of the ground like the kingdom of the mountain people, or—ooo, is it invisible like—”

“SILENCE!” Jesarin snapped, irked that her grand monologue was now ruined. “Since you don’t seem to value listening, I’ll make this simple. These are our lands and passage through them requires a toll. You appear woefully light in the pockets to provide the funds necessary, but since I am generous, I will only require your weapons and that pack of yours, and you can be on your way.”

Maribel’s eyes narrowed. “That doesn’t seem that generous.”

“On the contrary,” Jesarin smiled, “I’m letting you keep your lives, your most valuable asset. I assure you, it’s the best possible deal you can get.” She turned to the archer next to her. “Bollis, have the men secure their weapons, then—”

She didn’t finish, trailing off when she noticed his eye contact suddenly falter into a blank gaze. It likely had something to do with preceding thunk noise and the bolt sticking through his temples. As Bollis crumpled to the ground, Jesarin whirled back to see Maribel’s empty crossbow pointing at the former location of his head. Gresha stepped forward, gripping her battleaxe tightly. “No deal,” she grunted, lifting her lips to bare her sharp teeth.

Jesarin brandished her staff. “Kill the big one!” She yelled to her men. “I’ll take the midget.”

Gresha bellowed out a war cry as the bandits converged on her, but Maribel could only afford a quick glance in her direction before a streaking fireball launched from the end of Jesarin’s staff barreled toward her. Leaping to the side, she barely avoided the center of the smoldering patch of grass it created. Another fireball was sent at her, then a third, both expertly dodged by Maribel’s well-honed speed and agility. Taking cover behind a tree, the halfling loaded another bolt, then pivoted around to take the shot. Jesarin was ready however, and swung her staff in a wide arc in front of her. An arc of wind blasted forth, catching Maribel and sending her careening several off the ground and in a backspin. Her head smacked against the top of a boulder as she skidded across and landed behind it.

Disoriented with ears ringing, she could only hear the faint sounds of battle coming from Gresha and the other bandits. Reaching up and touching her aching scalp, she felt a warm trickle of blood, but when she brought her fingers back down to look, they were cloudy from the concussive impact of the rock. On the ground next to her, she caught the faint glow of a vial in her pack. Grabbing it and fumbling with the stopper, she popped it open and took a quick sip, hoping at least the throbbing would ease before Jesarin reached her.

Surprisingly, the pain, the ringing ears, and the blurred vision subsided almost instantly. Maribel sat up, already feeling better. “Wow, healing potions never work this fast, I wonder if this is an improved form—oooohhh what’s that tingle...?”

She experienced a similar sensation to a morning stretch, except more intense. Her leather corset began to ride up her torso and its laces loosen as her flesh beneath stretched and thickened. Running her hands over the newly-exposed skin on her stomach, she felt her abdominals tighten and form into a shallow cobblestone cluster. The cloth on her shirt sleeves and trousers started to strain from the increased mass pushing against it, causing tears to form over her now half-covered extended limbs. The soft moans escaping her lips deepened in tone and her face reformed from soft and childlike to angular and mature.

Jesarin approached the rock with the tip of her staff extended in front of her, crackling with magical energy. “Come out, little mouse,” she taunted. “I’ll make it nice and quick.” Rounding the side, she saw the hunched over blue-haired form and smiled malevolently, lifting and charging her weapon. Just before it fired, Maribel rolled around and shot an arm out, grabbing the staff and diverting away from her body. The fireball flared harmlessly into the air, unnoticed by the elf due to her eyes being fixed on the body rising to her feet, now double its former size and a head taller than her attacker. Jesarin tried to wrest the staff from the larger woman’s grip, but to no avail.

“Here I am, ‘little mouse’,” Maribel intoned, grasping the staff with her other hand and slamming the broad side of it against Jesarin’s face. Her neck snapped back and she dropped to the ground, out cold.

Now standing up straight, the former halfling took a moment to examine her form. Her shirt and trousers, once loose and comfortable, now clung tightly to her skin amidst large gashes in the fabric. The twin buns had come slightly loose and long strands hung freely down to the small of her back. The leather corset that once covered most of her upper body was now a bustier, with most of its laces snapped apart by her inflated chest sitting partially exposed and revealing shapely cleavage.

Maribel couldn’t help but giggle. “This is nice! Everything feels so...curvy!” Emerging from behind the rock, her excitement was momentarily halted when she caught a glimpse of Gresha, doubled over on her knees and using her axe to steady herself. The motionless bodies of six bandits were littered around her, various large wounds covering their bodies.

Stepping back and grabbing her pack, she rushed over to the orc, momentarily surprised by the larger strides she could now take. “Greshie—uh, I mean, Greshie!” she called out, trying to adjust her voice back to her original tone. “Are you ok?”

Gresha, still bent over with her back to Maribel, managed to let out a pained grunt. “Just a scratch.”

“Don’t worry, I’ll get you something!” Maribel reached into her pack and pulled out a pair of vials. Her eyes darted between the two as one glowed a little brighter than the other. Glancing back to her injured comrade, the side of her mouth turned up in a mischievous smirk.
143.title: Ambush!

info.brief_description: In the fantastical land of Amonar, a wizard crafts a beautifying potion, but chaos ensues
info.description:
This interactive story with a high fantasy genre will be heavily focused on female growth: namely, FMG, GTS, and BE.

There won’t be too many restrictions. Feel free to take the story in any direction you choose while staying relatively close to theme; exposition, plot, and lore-building are fine and even encouraged, but let’s not forget why we’re here...;)

I would prefer each chapter at least progress the story based on the option that lead to it in the previous chapter. Please avoid short, one or two sentence-long chapters only meant to transition to a new set of options.

Other than that...I dunno, no scat stuff, I guess?

Have fun!
